fr.recommendations: Ensuring that access routes to the main and secondary buildings are clearly marked and maintained could improve emergency responder access and navigation. The presence of multiple access points can facilitate timely arrival and diverse approach options for emergency vehicles, so maintaining these entrances free of obstructions is advised. The open terrain characteristic of the golf course and landscaped areas may support efficient staging and movement of responders, who can benefit from unobstructed sightlines and movement pathways. Clearly demarcated paths through the parking and patio areas could further aid in vehicle maneuverability and personnel mobility. Regularly updating emergency response plans to align with these property features may enhance overall operational effectiveness and responder safety.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the structure by ensuring adequate separation between vegetation and the property could reduce potential exposure. Managing the scattered trees by creating additional spacing between them may help to lower fuel continuity and decrease the potential for fire spread toward the structure. Ensuring that the area around the structure is free from combustible debris and maintaining an open terrain could support improved defensible space. The presence of an open fuel break can be beneficial for slowing wildfire progress, and preserving or expanding this feature where possible may further support property resilience. Evaluating the proximity of any tree clusters to the structure and increasing the clearance between them, while maintaining the overall health of the vegetation, could help reduce continuous fuel pathways. Consistent vegetation management beside access routes and within defensible space is essential to support firefighting operations and suppression activities effectively.
